reasoning: The evaluation identified four major defects across LLM fidelity and programmatic signals. Key issues include dropped double asterisks denoting 'Probable Action Items', flattened list hierarchies, retained header/footer elements, and table footnotes misformatted as bulleted lists. With exactly four major defects, the document requires HUMAN_REVIEW for remediation.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The source document uses a double asterisk (**) to denote "Probable Action Items". In several places, these double asterisks were dropped during extraction, which loses the specific meaning intended for those items. For example, 'Approval of Agenda **' became 'Approval of Agenda'. This occurs on multiple items across pages 1 and 2.

The output flattens the hierarchy of the list at the top of Page 2. In the source PDF, items like 'Observer Program' and 'Incidental Take Permit Updates' are nested as sub-bullets under 'Protected Resources Update', and 'Red Drum' and 'Southern Flounder' are nested under 'Landings Update'. In the JSON output, these third-level list items are extracted at the exact same hierarchical level as their parents (all are placed as direct children of 'Informational Materials continued:'). This loses the
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Protected Resources Update
 Observer Program
 Incidental Take Permit Updates

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (3): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=3. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commi". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

Several items in the agenda tables are bolded in the source document to indicate emphasis (action items, main headings like Adjourn). The output JSON fails to wrap these items in markdown asterisks to preserve the bold formatting.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "9:00 a.m."; 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Restore the double asterisks for all 'Probable Action Items'.
Correct the nesting of the list items under 'Protected Resources Update' and 'Landings Update' on Page 2.
Remove stray header and footer text from the main content body.
Reformat table footnotes as standard paragraphs rather than list items.
Add bold emphasis back to required table items such as action items and main headings.
